text: Code reviews are an essential practice in software development. When a developer completes a piece of code, a colleague reviews it before it is added to the main project. This process is very effective at catching bugs early, before they cause bigger problems later. Additionally, code reviews promote knowledge sharing across the team. Junior developers learn from more experienced colleagues, and everyone gains a better understanding of different parts of the codebase. This shared knowledge helps to build overall team quality, as the work is not dependent on just one person. In short, code reviews lead to better software and a stronger team.
